item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this in an early draft somewhere and I love how you made the robot from "robot that kills stuff" into "robot that is too serious about killing stuff." The testing log was cute and honestly made the article feel like a series 1. And even though it isn't a cliche I think the note at the end was nice touch. Props for using the cliches in such a creative and entertaining way.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm relatively new to this site, so I don't know if there you are the first author to posit a stand-off between Dr. Wondertainment and the GOC, but I like it and I am adding it to my head-cannon. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like SCP-1802, but without any of the subtlety that made its critique endearing, and being spoonfed exposition only increased its handfistedness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fucking LOVE the orange suit and pretty much upvoat anything that involves that God-tier weapons platform, even if it's a action figure sized version of it.
This is really creative, actually. I haven't read a skip in years that has even referenced any of the suits the GOC use, let alone a orange suit.
Very fun, Short, series 1-esque article and I wish there was more like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how Dr. Wondertainment seems to be getting a lot more angry and aggressive and fed up with the Foundation in this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew the moment I saw "Rage State" that this was a Cliche con entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done, short and sweet. As expressed by others I would like a couple extra tests, but it's pretty good as is. Also first time in a long time I've upvoted something that enters a "rage state". +1

item.title: Re: Never doubt a toymaker's spite.
(no title)
item.content:encoded:
a war between Wondertainment and GOC, with GOC trying to conduct itself seriously and Wondertainment basically just screwing around with them at every turn

I will carefully file this idea away in the mental vault and keep it very, very close. I like it a lot.

As to answer the testing question, I might once cliche-con is over, but I think the rules of the contest forbid me from doing it at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you have any plans of doing an extended testing log for this little guy? It definitely feels like an SCP that deserves one. I love the idea here of Wondertainment basically getting frustrated enough to create something to make fun of how ridiculously serious the GOC takes itself. As a matter of fact, I could even see this turning into a full blown series detailing a war between Wondertainment and GOC, with GOC trying to conduct itself seriously and Wondertainment basically just screwing around with them at every turn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation doesn't even use humanizing terms on actual humans much less a figurine.

That completely slipped by me, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a short but fun read. I wouldn't mind seeing more tests on it.

But this:

a small human figurine, who is entirely pink

The Foundation doesn't even use humanizing terms on actual humans much less a figurine.
I'd change the wording there. Still though, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that was a mistake, forgot to write "not" there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quick question: was this line "SCP-3871's definition of "anomalous" does only apply to actually anomalous objects as the Foundation knows them, as any object or phenomena that it perceives as being unnatural, even when perfectly in line with all known laws, will cause it to enter this rage state as well" supposed to read "does not only apply"? Because as it stands, it reads as a contradiction.

Other than that, well done with the rage state cliche. As with most Wondertainment products, this one really made me smile +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I'm imagining SCP-3871 attacking a researcher who did the thumb-pulling trick.

+1

item.title: Never doubt a toymaker's spite.
item.content:encoded:
My entry to Cliche-con 2019, (probably) falling under the topic of: "An item that is very good at doing the thing its supposed to do — too good, in fact."

SCP-3871 is a robot that is too good at destroying the unnatural and illogical, to the point where it elects to just blow up anything that isn't factually correct.

This was pretty fun to write.
